alarm.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:alert~ causes a popup dialog to appear, with the specified message. The browser then blocks until the dialog box is dismissed. See also ~l:confirm|v~ and ~l:prompt|v~.

befestige.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Each of the DOM variable types (~l:a~, ~l:div~, ~l:input~ etc) must be attached to an element already in the page and identified by its CSS id, before they can be used. Alternatively, you can ~l:create~ the element as a child of another DOM variable.%0a%0aThese two options allow you to build your page in HTML and attach _**AllSpeak**_ variables to the parts you want, or to build the whole structure in code and attach it to a single DOM element.%0a%0aIf you try to attach to a missing DOM element the system will throw an exception and you~sq~ll have to restart the script. To avoid this and stay in control, use the ~m:or~ clause to trap the exception and do something appropriate.
befestige.examples: befestige Container zu `ec-container`%0abefestige Test zu `ec-test`or alarm `Not found`

a.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a hyperlink variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

knopf/button.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<button>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

div.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<div>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

datei/file.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<file>~ variable, used in an image uploader. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.%0a%0aFor an example of uploading, see the File Manager in the [Here On The Map](https://hereonthemap.com) project.

formular/form.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<form>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h1.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<h1>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h2.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<h2>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h3.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<h3>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h4.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<h4>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h5.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<h5>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h6.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<h6>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

label.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<label>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

p.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<p>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

img.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares an ~m:<img>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

input.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares an ~m:<input>~ variable; a text entry field. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

ul.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<ul>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

li.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<li>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

select.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<select>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.%0a%0aThe ~m:select~ element is a dropdown list. There is no corresponding ~m:option~ element; instead, to construct the element you must create a JSON array containing the elements of the list, then use ~l:set!set Select from List~, where ~m:Select~ and ~m:List~ are the relevant variable types.

span.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<span>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

pre.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<pre>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

textfeld/textarea.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<textarea>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

audioclip.description:
[Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares an ~m:<audioclip>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

See also ~l:play~.

leere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Removes all the content from the named element.

konvertiere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This deals mainly with ~l:textarea~ data that contains line breaks, which render as %d%a sequences when used in emails, for example. This command replaces them with appropriate line breaks for printing or for use in HTML. Some experimentation will usually be needed in such cases.
konvertiere.examples: konvertiere leerraum in MyText zu drucke

erstelle.description:
[Übersetzung in Arbeit] 1. Creates a DOM element, adding it to the named parent (which must already exist in the DOM). If ~m:in {parent}~ is missing the element will be created in the ~m:<body>~ of the document.

2. Creates an ~l:audioclip~ from any reachable URL.

When you create an element it will be given an id with this pattern:

~m:ec-{name}-{index}-{id}~

where ~m:{name}~ is the name of the variable, ~m:index~ the current ~l:index|k|core~ of the variable and ~m:id~ an incrementing value. If you examine the DOM using the developer tools of your browser you will see this pattern clearly.
erstelle.examples:
erstelle MyButton in Container
create Buzz von `https://sample.com/audio/buzzer.mp3`

deaktiviere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Sets the ~m:disabled~ property of the named DOM variable to ~m:true~. See ~l:enable~.

aktiviere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Sets the ~m:disabled~ property of the named DOM variable to ~m:false~. See ~l:disable~.

hole.description:
[Übersetzung in Arbeit] 1. Gets a value from the browser local storage, accessed by its key name. Browser storage is very useful for remembering settings from a previous visit to the page. See also ~l:put!put into storage~. Web storage is per origin (domain and protocol). All pages from one origin can store and access the same data. If you omit the ~m:as {key}~ part the function will return an array comprising all the keys currently held in local storage.

2. Gets all the field data from the named form as a JSON string. Elements such as ~l:textarea~ will have line breaks replaced by ~m:“&0a;”~ to prevent them causing problems elsewhere, so you may need to ~l:convert~ them later on.
hole.examples:
hole Mode von speicher als `mode-assisted`
get TheData von MyForm

section.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<section>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

verlauf.description:
[Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:history~ manipulates the browser history stack. This is a complex and rather confusing subject so it's best to study examples.

~m:set~ and ~m:replace~ are identical. They 'mark' the history stack to provide a point to return to when the user clicks the browser Back button.

~m:pop~ and ~m:back~ are also identical. They have the same effect as clicking the browser Back button.

~m:push~ adds a new frame to the browser history stack.

The ~m:url~value contains the SEO string to add to the browser's address bar.

The ~m:state~ value typically contains a JSON string with enough information to restore the state to what it was prior to calling ~m:push~.

If you use ~m:pop~, don't be tempted to add any other functionality because if the user clicks Back the script won't know anything about it.

See also ~l:json parse|k|json!parse~ for dealing with URLs.
verlauf.examples:
verlauf setze zustand CurrentState
history stapele url `help` zustand State
history zurück/zurueck

ort.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Go to a new location. Sets the browser address to the given ~m:url~; a kind of ~m:GOTO~ for the browser.%0a%0aIf ~m:new~ is included the location will open in a new browser tab.

mail.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This requests the system to create a new email, addressed to the named ~m:recipient~, with the given ~m:subject~ and containing the given ~m:message~. The user can then complete the email and send it.%0a%0aThere is no mechanism in JavaScript for sending emails directly. This is one way around it; the other is to use the REST server with this endpoint:%0a%0a~m:{site root}/static-hosting: no server email endpoint~%0a%0aThe endpoint expects a payload to be provided in the form of a JSON object with the fields ~m:from~, ~m:to~, ~m:subject~ and ~m:message~.

auf.syntax:
auf änderung/aenderung {list/radiobutton} ...
on klick dokument ...
on klick {element} ...
on schlüssel/schluessel/taste ...
on browser zurück/zurueck ...
on wische links/rechts ...
on verlassen ...
on wähle/waehle/ziehe/ablege ...%0aauf fenster größenänderung/groessenaenderung ...%0aauf fortsetze ...
auf.description:
[Übersetzung in Arbeit] Handles a selection in a list, a click on any DOM element, a keypress or a swipe left or right in a mobile browser.

The ~m:on click document~ variant deals with the case where you have imported HTML into your page from another source. This variant detects a click in any ~m:<a>~ element as long as somewhere in the DOM hierarchy above the link there is an element with an id starting with ~m:ec-~. Any element created using the ~l:create~ command will give the new element an id fitting this pattern. You can find which link was click using ~l:document path|v![the] document path~.

The ~m:on change~ variant is used with lists and radio buttons.

The ~m:on leave~ variant allows you to take an action when the visitor leaves your page, such as writing to a tracking log. Please note that your browser is unlikely to let you take any action that will block the leave action (such as putting up a dialog).

The variants ~m:on pick~, ~m:on drag~ and ~m:on drop~ handle drag and drop. The first is triggered when you select the named component, the second when you move the selected component and the third when you finally release it at its new position. Drag and drop is the subject of a couple of examples in the Codex Tutorial, that you can see by clicking the **Tutorial** button above. See also ~l:position|v~.

See also ~l:on|k|core~ in the ~l:on|k|core!core package~.%0a%0aThe ~m:on window resize~ variant fires whenever the browser window is resized.%0a%0aThe ~m:on resume~ variant fires when the page regains visibility after being hidden (e.g. the user switches back to the tab).
auf.examples:
auf änderung/aenderung Countries gehe zu SelectCountry
on klick HelpButton gehe zu ShowHelp
on klick dokument gehe zu ShowHelp
on schlüssel/schluessel/taste gehe zu ProcessKey
on browser zurück/zurueck gehe zu RestorePage
on wische links gehe zu Next
on verlassen sende `leave` zu TrackingModule
on wähle/waehle Component gehe zu DoPick
on ziehe gehe zu DoDrag
on ablege gehe zu DoDrop

lege.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Puts an item of data into browser local storage, where it can be retrieved at any time by the same computer using the same page. See also ~l:get!get from storage~.%0a%0aWeb storage is per origin (domain and protocol). All pages from one origin can store and access the same data.
lege.examples: lege `Admin` in speicher als UserName%0alege `https://google.com` in speicher als SearchURL

entferne.syntax: entferne element {element}%0aentferne attribut {name} von {element}%0aentferne {key} von speicher
entferne.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] 1. This removes a named DOM element from its parent~sq~s child list.%0a%0a2. This removes an attribute of an element, such as when manipulating selections in lists.%0a%0a3. This removes an item from local storage.
entferne.examples: entferne element HelpDiv%0aentferne attribut `checked` von ListItem%0aentferne `password` von speicher

scrolle.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Scrolls an element or the browser window to the position requested. For a window, the position to scroll to, if not zero, will normally have been previously saved using ~l:scroll position|v![the] scroll position~.
scrolle.examples: scrolle zu 0%0ascrolle Panel zu 100%0ascrolle zu SavedScrollPosition

setze.description:
[Übersetzung in Arbeit] The ~m:set~ command is the most heavily used in _**AllSpeak**_ (and in English generally). The examples above cover all of the different variants. In many of them, ~m:the~ is optional, but it's always best to include it as an aid to readability.

Also note that the only way to assign classes to elements is by using ~m:set attribute~. All elements are given ids when they are ~l:create!created~.

The form ~m:set {select} from {list} as {selected}~ loads a ~l:select|k|browser~ element from a json array and optionally sets one of them to be the selected value. Note that the values must be strings; if you are using numbers then add them to the list as ~m:`` cat N~ to force them to become strings.

~m:set~ is also used in other packages, for example in ~l:set|k|core!core~.
setze.examples:
setze der/die/das/den/dem/des inhalt von TargetDiv von SourceDiv
set der/die/das/den/dem/des titel zu `My First AllSpeak Page`
set der/die/das/den/dem/des inhalt von Link zu `Click für/fuer more`
set der/die/das/den/dem/des id von ContentDiv zu `ec-contents`
set der/die/das/den/dem/des text von HelpButton zu `Help`
set der/die/das/den/dem/des größe/groesse von NameField zu 40
set attribut `class` von MainText zu `normal-text`
set stil `display` von ButtonPanel zu `none`
set stil `text-align` von körper/koerper zu `center`
set der/die/das/den/dem/des stile von TextPanel zu `flex:2;font-weight:bold;margin-top:1em`
set der/die/das/den/dem/des standard von Countries zu `Spain`
set Months von MonthList als `March`

verfolge.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Sets up program tracing or acts as a breakpoint. In the former case it is given a list of variables whose values it should display. In the second case, program execution stops and the user interacts with on-screen buttons to resume or step one command at a time. Tracing is a debugging feature and requires a special DIV to be included in the page:%0a%0a~m:<div id="allspeak-tracer"></div>~%0a%0aThis is where it places its buttons and display panel.%0a%0aYou can choose whether to display variables inline or on separate lines, by specifying ~m:horizontal~ (the default) or ~m:vertical~. This follows the list of variables.%0a%0aWhen the breakpoint is hit the display panel becomes visible, showing the current values of all the variables you asked to display, followed by the lines of script leading up to where you stopped. There are 2 buttons above the display; ~m:Run~ restarts the program from the current point and ~m:Step~ advances to the next script instruction.
verfolge.examples: verfolge Value1 Value2 horizontal%0averfolge Width Height Count vertikal%0averfolge

hochlade.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This command is a standard image uploader with a progress bar. The file to be uploaded will be in a ~l:file~ element and progess is monitored with a ~l:progress~ element. Progress, in the form of percentage complete, is also written to the ~l:span!status~ element given, as are error messages (if any).%0a%0aThe command uses a special REST endpoint to write the file to the server at a location starting with a directory called ~m:resources~ at the top of your WordPress installation. The ~m:path~ value is relative to that. Any intermediate directories will be created automatically if they don~sq~t already exist.%0a%0aImages will be scaled to a maximum of 1024 pixels wide or high before being saved in the server.
hochlade.examples: hochlade UploadFile zu Path mit UploadProgress und UploadStatus

sortiere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This is a powerful generalized JSON array sorter. Like the JavaScript function that runs internally, all the work is done by a custom function, here provided by the script.%0a%0aThe sort function sets up pairs of array elements to be compared by the comparator function, putting them into ~m:a~ and ~m:b~ arguments. The result of the comparison (1, 0 or -1) goes into the ~m:v~ argument. The example above is a complete script that you can run in the [AllSpeak Codex](https://codex.allspeak.software). See also ~l:filter~.
sortiere.examples: script SortTest%0a    %0a  variable Items%0a  variable A%0a  variable B%0a  variable V%0a    %0a  put leer in Items%0a  append `fish` zu Items%0a  append `cabbage` zu Items%0a  append `sugar` zu Items%0a  append `beef` zu Items%0a  append `potatoes` zu Items%0a  sort Items mit Comparator%0a  alert Items%0a  exit%0a%0aComparator:%0a  put arg `a` von Items in A%0a  put arg `b` von Items in B%0a  if A ist größer/groesser als B lege 1 in V%0a  else wenn A ist kleiner als B lege -1 in V%0a  else lege 0 in V%0a  set arg `v` von Items zu V%0a  stop

filtere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This is a powerful generalized JSON array filter. Like the JavaScript function that runs internally, all the work is done by a custom function, here provided by the script.%0a%0aThe filter command sets up each array element to be examined in turn by the filter function, putting it into the ~m:a~ argument. The result of the filter (true or false) goes into the ~m:v~ argument. The example above is a complete script that you can run in the [AllSpeak Codex](https://codex.allspeak.software). See also ~l:sort~.
filtere.examples: script FilterTest%0a    %0a  variable Items%0a  variable A%0a  variable V%0a    %0a  put leer in Items%0a  append `fish` zu Items%0a  append `cabbage` zu Items%0a  append `sugar` zu Items%0a  append `beef` zu Items%0a  append `potatoes` zu Items%0a  filter Items mit MyFilter%0a  alert Items%0a  exit%0a%0aMyFilter:%0a  put arg `a` von Items in A%0a  if der/die/das/den/dem/des länge/laenge von A ist größer/groesser als 5 setze V sonst leere V%0a  set arg `v` von Items zu V%0a  stop

fordere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This manages fullscreen mode, where the content of the document occupies the entire screen with now browser element being visible.%0a%0aThis feature allows **_AllSpeak_** to be used for display systems where the whole screen is used, with no title bar or other window furniture visible.%0a%0aNote that if you go into fullscreen mode you may need to provide some means of returning, as the browser Back button will not be visible.

fokus.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Requests the focus to be applied to the field whose name is given.

BROWSER.syntax: Language features relating zu der/die/das/den/dem/des browser environment.
BROWSER.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] These commands handle the browser environment, dealing with DOM objects, their properties and their behaviours.

blockquote.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declares a DOM blockquote element variable.

canvas.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declares a DOM canvas element variable.

fieldset.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declares a DOM fieldset element variable.

hr.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declares a DOM hr element variable.

image.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declares a DOM image element variable.

legend.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declares a DOM legend element variable.

option.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declares a DOM option element variable.

progress.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declares a DOM progress element variable.

table.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declares a DOM table element variable.

td.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declares a DOM td element variable.

th.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declares a DOM th element variable.

tr.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declares a DOM tr element variable.

klick.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Programmatically dispatches a click event on the specified select element.

kopiere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Selects and copies the content of an input element to the clipboard.

markiere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Selects (highlights) the content of the specified DOM element.

spiele.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Plays the specified audioclip element.

rendere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Renders a Webson script into the specified DOM element.

{element}.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the ~sq~value~sq~ of the element. For a ~l:select~ this is the selected item; for a ~l:file~, ~l:input~ or  ~l:textarea~ it is the text content of the element.
{element}.examples: lege TheFile in Value%0awenn NameInput ist Admin alarm `Invalid name`%0alege Countries in Country%0awenn Description ist leer gehe zu Default

existiert.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if the ~m:element~ in question exists.
existiert.examples: wenn Price existiert addiere Price zu Total

mobil.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:mobile~ has the value ~m:true~ if the system is a smartphone. See also ~l:mobile|c~ in ~l:mobile|c!Conditions~.

br.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the value ~m:<br />~, the HTML line break tag.
br.examples: lege `Line 1` cat br cat `Line 2` in Panel

attribut.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Gets the named attribute from an element. See also ~l:set|k!set attribute~.
attribut.examples: lege attribut `class` von TheElement in Class

bestätige/bestaetige.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Causes a confirmation dialog to appear with the prompt text given and buttons ~m:OK~ and ~m:Cancel~. If the user clicks ~m:OK~ the value returned is ~m:true~, otherwise ~m:false~. See also ~l:alert|k~ and ~l:prompt~.
bestätige/bestaetige.examples: wenn bestätige/bestaetige `Are you sure?` gehe zu Continue

eingabeaufforderung.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Causes a dialog box to appear asking for input. The response box is preloaded with the text given. If the user clicks ~m:Cancel~ an empty string is returned. See also ~l:alert|k~ and ~l:confirm~.
eingabeaufforderung.examples: lege eingabeaufforderung `Please give a reason:` mit `Rejected` in Reason

farbe.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Creates a color item of the form ~m:#abcdef~ from the supplied numeric value.

docpath.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This is used with ~l:on|k!on click document~ to trap clicks on all hyperlinks in a block of HTML that was loaded from outside the _**AllSpeak**_ environment. The value returned is the URL minus its domain portion.

inhalt von.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Gets the content of the named element. Some elements can also use ~l:text of![the] text of~ as an alternative syntax.
inhalt von.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des inhalt von AddressField in Address

text von.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Gets the content of the named element. This is an alternative to ~l:content of![the] text of~.
text von.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des text von AddressField in Address

breite.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Gets the width of the named element, relative to the viewport (window, or in the case of mobile, the screen). See also ~l:left~, ~l:right~, ~l:top~, ~l:bottom~ and ~l:height~.
breite.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des breite von Image in Width

höhe/hoehe.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Gets the height of the named element, relative to the viewport (window, or in the case of mobile, the screen). See also ~l:left~, ~l:right~, ~l:top~, ~l:bottom~ and ~l:width~.
höhe/hoehe.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des höhe/hoehe von Image in Height

ort.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the current location, i.e. the URL of the current page.

schlüssel/schluessel/taste.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the key last pressed. Used in an ~l:on|k!on key~ handler.

scrolle position.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the current scroll position of the browser window, which can be used to restore it to the same position later - see ~l:scroll|kscroll!scroll to {position}~.
scrolle position.examples: Put der/die/das/den/dem/des scrolle position in ScrollPos

elternteil.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns either the name or the ~l:index|k~ of the element inside which a click occurs when using ~l:on|k!on click in document~.
elternteil.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des elternteil name in Parent%0alege der/die/das/den/dem/des elternteil index in Index

verlauf zustand.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the current history state from the browser history stack. See ~l:history|k~.
verlauf zustand.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des verlauf zustand in State

hostname.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Gets the ~sq~hostname~sq~ property of the current browser location.
hostname.examples: lege hostname in HostName%0awenn der/die/das/den/dem/des hostname ist `localhost` alarm `Running locally!`

bildschirm.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Gets the screen width or height
bildschirm.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des bildschirm breite in W%0alege der/die/das/den/dem/des bildschirm höhe/hoehe in H

links.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Gets the position of the leftmost edge of the named element, relative to the viewport (window, or in the case of mobile, the screen). %0a%0aIf ~m:offset~ is given the value returned is the position relative to the component~sq~s immediate parent container.%0a%0aSee also ~l:right~, ~l:top~, ~l:bottom~, ~l:width~, ~l:height~ and ~l:position~.
links.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des links von MyImage in Left%0alege der/die/das/den/dem/des versatz links von Text in TextLeft

rechts.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Gets the position of the rightmost edge of the named element, relative to the viewport (window, or in the case of mobile, the screen). See also ~l:left~, ~l:top~, ~l:bottom~, ~l:width~ and ~l:height~.
rechts.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des rechts von Image in Right

oben.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Gets the position of the top edge of the named element, relative to the viewport (window, or in the case of mobile, the screen). %0a%0aIf ~m:offset~ is given the value returned is the position relative to the component~sq~s immediate parent container.%0a%0aSee also ~l:left~, ~l:right~, ~l:bottom~, ~l:width~, ~l:height~ and ~l:position~.
oben.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des oben von Image in Top%0alege der/die/das/den/dem/des versatz oben von Text in TextTop

unten.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Gets the position of the bottom of the named element, relative to the viewport (window, or in the case of mobile, the screen). See also ~l:left~, ~l:right~, ~l:top~, ~l:width~ and ~l:height~.
unten.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des unten von Image in Bottom

position.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The retrieves the position of the last mouse click, or of the position while dragging a component. The returned value is a JSON string with properties ~m:x~ and ~m:y~.%0a%0aThese values are useful when implementing drag-and-drop (see ~l:on|k~) as they tell you how far the component has been moved from its starting position. See also ~l:left~ and ~l:top~.
position.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des wähle/waehle position in PickPos%0alege der/die/das/den/dem/des ziehe position in DragPos

ausgewählt/ausgewaehlt.syntax: [der/die/das/den/dem/des] ausgewählt/ausgewaehlt index in {list}%0a[der/die/das/den/dem/des] ausgewählt/ausgewaehlt element in {list}
ausgewählt/ausgewaehlt.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the index of the selected item in a ~l:select~ element, or the item itself.
ausgewählt/ausgewaehlt.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des ausgewählt/ausgewaehlt index in Countries in Index%0alege der/die/das/den/dem/des ausgewählt/ausgewaehlt element in Countries in Country

browser name.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the name of the current browser (e.g. Chrome, Firefox, Safari).

klick position.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the left (x) or top (y) coordinate of the last click event.

ziehe position.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the current drag position as a JSON object with x and y properties.

querformat.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns true if the viewport width is greater than or equal to its height.

wähle/waehle position.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the current pick position as a JSON object with x and y properties.

hochformat.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns true if the viewport height is greater than its width.

speicher schlüssel/schluessel.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns a JSON array of all localStorage key names.

stil von.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the value of the specified CSS style property of a DOM element.
stil von.examples: lege stil `color` von MyDiv in Color

bestätige/bestaetige.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Puts up a confirmation dialog with the prompt message given. If the user clicks OK the result is ~m:true~, otherwise ~m:false~.
bestätige/bestaetige.examples: wenn bestätige/bestaetige `Are you sure, ` cat MyName gehe zu DoIt

has [der/die/das/den/dem/des] fokus.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if the specified element has the focus.
has [der/die/das/den/dem/des] fokus.examples: wenn element NameInput has der/die/das/den/dem/des fokus gehe zu ClearName

mobil.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if the browser is running on a smartphone.

existiert.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if the ~m:element~ in question exists.
existiert.examples: wenn Price existiert addiere Price zu Total

enthält/enthaelt.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if the bounding rectangle of the element contains the given position (JSON with x, y).

alarm.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:alert~ causes a popup dialog to appear, with the specified message. The browser then blocks until the dialog box is dismissed.

befestige.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Each of the DOM variable types (~l:a~, ~l:div~, ~l:input~ etc) must be attached to an element already in the page and identified by its CSS id, before they can be used. Alternatively, you can ~l:create~ the element as a child of another DOM variable.%0a%0aThese two options allow you to build your page in HTML and attach _**AllSpeak**_ variables to the parts you want, or to build the whole structure in code and attach it to a single DOM element.

a.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a hyperlink variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

knopf/button.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<button>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

div.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<div>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

datei/file.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<file>~ variable, used in an image uploader. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.%0a%0aFor an example of uploading, see the File Manager in the [Here On The Map](https://hereonthemap.com) project.

formular/form.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<form>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h1.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<h1>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h2.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<h2>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h3.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<h3>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h4.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<h4>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h5.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<h5>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h6.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<h6>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

label.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<label>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

p.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<p>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

img.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares an ~m:<img>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

input.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares an ~m:<input>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

ul.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<ul>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

li.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<li>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

select.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<select>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

span.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<span>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

pre.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<pre>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

popup.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<popup>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.%0a%0aA variable type to represent a section of HTML code as a popup that floats above the page and disappears when the user clicks outside its main area. These are used primarily for menus.

textfeld/textarea.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<textarea>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

audioclip.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares an ~m:<audioclip>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.%0a%0aSee also ~l:play~.

leere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Removes all the content from the named element.

konvertiere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This deals mainly with ~l:textarea~ data that contains line breaks, which render as %d%a sequences when used in emails, for example. This command replaces them with appropriate line breaks for printing or for use in HTML. Some experimentation will usually be needed in such cases.
konvertiere.examples: konvertiere leerraum in MyText zu drucke

erstelle.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] 1. Creates a DOM object, adding it to the named parent (which must already exist in the DOM).%0a%0a2. Creates an ~l:audioclip~ from any reachable URL.
erstelle.examples: erstelle MyButton in Container%0aerstelle Buzz von `https://sample.com/audio/buzzer.mp3`

deaktiviere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Sets the ~m:disabled~ property of the named DOM variable to ~m:true~. See ~l:enable~.

aktiviere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Sets the ~m:disabled~ property of the named DOM variable to ~m:false~. See ~l:disable~.

hole.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] 1. Gets a value from the browser storage, accessed by its key name. Browser storage is very useful for remembering settings from a previous visit to the page. See also ~l:put!put into storage~. Web storage is per origin (domain and protocol). All pages from one origin can store and access the same data.%0a%0a2. Gets all the field data from the named form as a JSON string. Elements such as ~l:textarea~ will have line breaks replaced by ~m:“&0a;”~ to prevent them causing problems elsewhere, so you may need to ~l:convert~ them later on.
hole.examples: hole Mode von speicher als `mode-assisted`%0ahole TheData von MyForm

section.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This declares a ~m:<section>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

verlauf.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:history~ manipulates the browser history stack. This is a complex and rather confusing subject so it~sq~s best to study examples.%0a%0a~m:set~ and ~m:replace~ are identical. They ~sq~mark~sq~ the history stack to provide a point to return to when the user clicks the browser Back button.%0a%0a~m:pop~ and ~m:back~ are also identical. They have the same effect as clicking the browser Back button.%0a%0a~m:push~ adds a new frame to the browser history stack.%0a%0aThe ~m:url~value contains the SEO string to add to the browser~sq~s address bar.%0a%0aThe ~m:state~ value typically contains a JSON string with enough information to restore the state to what it was prior to calling ~m:push~.%0a%0aIf you use ~m:pop~, don~sq~t be tempted to add any other functionality because if the user clicks Back the script won~sq~t know anything about it.
verlauf.examples: verlauf setze zustand CurrentState%0averlauf stapele url `help` zustand State%0averlauf zurück/zurueck

ort.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Go to a new location. Sets the browser address to the given ~m:url~; a kind of ~m:GOTO~ for the browser.

mail.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This requests the system to create a new email, addressed to the named ~m:recipient~, with the given ~m:subject~ and containing the given ~m:message~. The user can then complete the email and send it.%0a%0aThere is no mechanism in JavaScript for sending emails directly. This is one way around it; the other is to use the REST server with this endpoint:%0a%0a~m:{site root}/static-hosting: no server email endpoint~%0a%0aThe endpoint expects a payload to be provided in the form of a JSON object with the fields ~m:from~, ~m:to~, ~m:subject~ and ~m:message~.

auf.syntax: auf änderung/aenderung {list} ...%0aauf klick dokument ...%0aauf klick {element} ...%0aauf schlüssel/schluessel/taste ...%0aauf browser zurück/zurueck ...%0aauf wische links/rechts ...
auf.examples: auf änderung/aenderung Countries gehe zu SelectCountry%0aauf klick HelpButton gehe zu ShowHelp%0aauf klick dokument gehe zu ShowHelp%0aauf schlüssel/schluessel/taste gehe zu ProcessKey%0aauf browser zurück/zurueck gehe zu RestorePage%0aauf wische links gehe zu Next

lege.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Puts an item of data into browser local storage, where it can be retrieved at any time by the same computer using the same page. See also ~l:get!get from storage~.%0a%0aWeb storage is per origin (domain and protocol). All pages from one origin can store and access the same data.
lege.examples: lege `Admin` in speicher als UserName%0alege `https://google.com` in speicher als SearchURL
